title: Crash Handling & Error Reporting
description: Uncaught exception capture, mod-cause identification, crash report file generation, and user notification
entry_points: ServerLauncher.main() wraps HeadlessApplication with lambda: throwable -> CrashHandler.handle(throwable, f -> {}); outer try/catch also calls CrashHandler.handle(t, f -> {})
activity_flow: START: Uncaught exception thrown at runtime
[Android/iOS] → CrashHandler.log(exception) → createReport() → write to crashes/crash_<ts>.txt → END (no UI)
STEP 1: Analyse error strings for GPU/window init failure
DECISION: total.contains('Couldn\'t create window') OR 'OpenGL 2.0' OR 'pixel format' OR 'GLEW'?
YES → message(GPU/graphics error string); set badGPU=true
STEP 2: CrashHandler.getModCause(e) → returns LoadedMod or null
YES → causeString = '<ModName> has caused Mindustry to crash...'
YES → causeString = 'A mod or script has caused Mindustry to crash...'
STEP B: settings.manualSave()  [attempt to save game data]
YES → System.exit(1)  [skip crash report for dev builds]
STEP E: Build crash report file path: crashes/crash-report-<MM_dd_yyyy_HH_mm_ss>.txt
STEP G: CrashHandler.createReport(exception) → write to file
STEP H: writeListener.get(file)  [callback with File reference]
STEP i:   writeException(exception) → stack trace string via StringWriter/PrintWriter
STEP ii:  getModCause(exception) → scan stack frames; match against mods.list() by class name prefix

STEP iii: Build report string: header (mod or vanilla), report URL if no mods + not custom build, version info, date, OS, GL version, Java version, memory, cores, likely cause, enabled mods list, patches, stack trace
YES → message(causeString + '\nThe logs have been saved in:\n' + file.getAbsolutePath() + ...)
NO  → skip message (GPU error already shown)
--- ErrorDialog.show(text) flow (used for pre-crash checks, e.g. bad Java/32-bit) ---
CrashHandler.handle(t, f -> {})  [empty writeListener — no user dialog on headless server]
All steps A-J execute; no UI message displayed
ServerControl itself does not directly call CrashHandler; it relies on the HeadlessApplication crash callback registered in ServerLauncher
key_methods: CrashHandler.handle(Throwable exception, Cons<File> writeListener): void — full crash pipeline
CrashHandler.log(Throwable exception): void — silent log-only path (Android/iOS)
CrashHandler.createReport(Throwable exception): String — builds full report string
CrashHandler.getModCause(Throwable e): LoadedMod? — stack-trace mod attribution
CrashHandler.getMatches(String name1, String name2): int — package prefix match count
CrashHandler.writeException(Throwable e): String — stack trace to string
DesktopLauncher.handleCrash(Throwable e): void — desktop-specific crash entry with GPU check
ErrorDialog.show(String text): void — SDL/Swing modal error dialog + System.exit(1)
conditions_and_branches: Dev build skip: username == 'anuke' && modifier != 'steam' → immediate exit, no report written
GPU/graphics error (desktop): detected from exception message → show GPU error, skip causeString message
Mod cause (desktop + report): stack frame class doesn't match core packages → matched against mod.meta.main prefix
JS mod cause: stack frame filename ends with .js and starts with mod.name + '/' → attributed to that mod
Rhino scripting error (desktop): stack contains 'rhino.gen.' → generic mod/script message
No mods + official build: append reportIssueURL to report
Version not yet loaded: fall back to reading /version.properties before writing report
32-bit Windows (desktop pre-launch): ErrorDialog.show(...) → immediate exit before crash handler needed
Old Java version (desktop pre-launch): ErrorDialog.show(...) → immediate exit
key_relationships: ServerLauncher registers CrashHandler.handle as HeadlessApplication crash callback
AndroidLauncher depends_on CrashHandler.log (no full handle pipeline)
CrashHandler.handle calls createReport → writes crash file; calls writeListener callback
DesktopLauncher.handleCrash calls ErrorDialog.show indirectly via 'message()' (same SDL/Swing path)
ErrorDialog.show is also called directly for pre-flight checks (Java version, 32-bit)
ServerControl is an ApplicationListener registered by ServerLauncher; crash propagates up to HeadlessApplication callback
output_artifacts: User dialog (desktop): SDL message box or JOptionPane modal
important_notes: Both desktop and server ultimately call System.exit(1) after reporting
CrashHandler is a pure static utility class (no instantiation)
ErrorDialog is desktop-only; server path has no UI notification
The writeListener pattern decouples file-write from notification (server uses no-op lambda)
Mod attribution uses package-prefix matching; JS mods matched by filename pattern
settings.manualSave() is attempted before exit to preserve game state
